verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a Type I single-pass transmembrane protein with an extracellular ectodomain. In acrosome-intact sperm it resides in the acrosomal membrane (inner and outer leaflets). Upon acrosome reaction, it translocates to the plasma membrane of the equatorial segment, where it is surface-exposed and binds the egg receptor JUNO to mediate sperm-egg fusion. The protein body reaches the outer leaflet by its own transmembrane anchor, but this surface exposure is strictly conditioned on completion of the acrosome reaction. Verdict is contextual/cell_state_induced.
